programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The stakes are high as the Unsolved Crime and Open Case Squad goes after one of the biggest crime families in London, and it's those closest to Gerry who are dangerously put in the firing line. Starring Dennis Waterman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness

programme.desc: Danny and Steve 'welcome' new boss Ted Case into the UCOS office in Sasha's absence, but for all his experience, keen eye for detail and a killer instinct for detecting liars, they aren't as sold on his superstitious quirks. While trying to work one another out, the team treads carefully in the case of a murdered vicar who received racist hate mail in the lead up to his death. programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A middle-class couple's adopted child is kidnapped, a case that takes Gently and Bacchus to a home where unmarried mothers are forced to give up their babies. Suspicion initially falls upon the natural mother, who is thought to have 'stolen' her baby back. But further investigations reveal a much darker side to the adoption home itself and raise questions as to how far this seemingly perfect couple were prepared to go to become parents. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Mark Gatiss, Helen Baxendale and Alison Steadman.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ted leads an investigation into the apparent suicide of a city trader who was embroiled in the cut-throat world of London's financial district in the 1980s. As the team delve deeper into the macho fraternity of 'The Wolfpack', they find a darker side to the debauchery, secret hand signals and all-nighters that bonded the traders together - including a strict hierarchy that might be worth killing for. Meanwhile, Steve tries to learn some tricks of the trade, amid his own financial crisis. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team pursues a serial killer with a grisly desire to look at his victims' faces as they die, and the trail leads into the world of academic philosophy. However, Nikki is distracted from the case by the death of her grandmother, while Harry investigates the demise of a woman who apparently drowned in three inches of water.(n)

programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael struggle to regain each other's trust, and a show producer has to step in to try and repair the damage. Meanwhile, Michael gets drawn deeper into the situation with his neighbour, and David is worried about Georgia's plans to help a friend who is in labour. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at some of the best moments from the Michael McIntyre's Big Show series, revisiting some of the biggest laughs from the past five seasons. Tonight, he looks back on the hilarious Send To All's from Doctor Who star Bradley Walsh and comedian Alan Carr. Chef Gino D'Acampo gets a rude awakening to play the Midnight Gameshow, and one audience member plays a game of Soap Or Nope - and receives her very own cliffhanger when she meets her pen-pal of 70 years for the very first time.(n)

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: Newly qualified physiotherapist Esther dreads her forthcoming move from the neurology department to outpatients and the proposed switch is causing her some worrying health problems. Occupational therapist Melissa cares deeply for her patients, but her dyslexia and the demands of essential paperwork means she's losing motivation for her job.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams vie at Brighton Racecourse, East Sussex, hunting for items of value with the help of experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey. Presenter Christina Trevanion maintains order and also shows off a tasty mystery item.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull travel around Britain as they explore their favourite scientific breakthroughs, beginning with the 18th-century inventions that kick-started the Industrial Revolution and transformed scientific understanding. They head to Derbyshire to investigate Richard Arkwright's water frame - a spinning machine that allowed cotton textiles to be mass-produced, and then examine James Watt's separate condenser, an unassuming invention that would be the biggest single improvement ever made to the steam engine.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament presented by Alicia McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Life in the tropical Atlantic, where everything from dolphins, manatees and whale sharks to sunbathing jellyfish thrive in the Caribbean's warm, sheltered waters, fringed by coral reefs and rich mangrove forests. But when extreme heat in Africa unleashes terrifying hurricanes, all hell breaks loose across the region.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation in Scarborough, North Yorkshire. In their quest to find the best local food, they visit a fish and chip cafe with a glamorous history, go on a train ride and attend a tea-dance with more than 30 different varieties of cake for them to sample.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Netherlands v France. A chance to see the Euro 2008 Group C encounter at Stade de Suisse in Bern, Switzerland.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from East Yorkshire to Caernarfon, beginning by travelling from the city of Hull to the North Yorkshire market town of Malton.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.sub-title: A Moment in the Sun: The Terrestrial Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox tells the life story of the solar system, beginning with the development of the four rocky worlds closest to the Sun - Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars. They were born at the same time from the same material - yet have lived radically different lives. What immense forces are at play?(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Typhoo Tea factory on the Wirral, which he visited back in 2017, where production has been upped to produce 109 million bags a week. In reports from the programme's archives, Cherry Healey visits a tea processing plant in Kenya, and Ruth Goodman sheds light on how Second World War troops risked their lives to brew up.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monaco's only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanie's daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros' worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Tim Key performs his isolation poetry, Morgana Robinson showcases her impersonation of Cheryl Cole and Charlie Higson brings The Fast Show's Swiss Toni back to life. Plus, Bob Mortimer shows what it is possible to create from a slab of butter, a tin of sardines and an orange.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, London Hughes, Rhys James and Milton Jones join regular panellist Hugh Dennis on the comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the house's history from the 1880s to the First World War, a period that saw the property sliding down the social scale and turning from a family home to a multiple occupancy. He discovers how a resident responded to the death of his baby daughter by opening a milk bar for the temperance movement, only to get into legal trouble for watering down his product. David also discovers the story behind a mysterious occupant who lived in the house under a pseudonym and learns how the battle of Passchendaele changed the residents' lives forever.(n)

programme.title: Soon Gone: A Windrush Chronicle
programme.desc: Cyrus Williams believes that he has found the love of his life after a chance meeting during a hospital appointment, but finds the disapproval of his friends and the constraints of living in England place a strain on the relationship.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Steve Brown explores Elmley Nature Reserve on the Isle of Sheppey, where swallows and swifts are gathering in their hordes, heralding the arrival of summer. Margherita Taylor is on the hunt for an invasive species of moth that threatens not just the oaks on London's Hampstead Heath, but walkers too. On his farm, Adam Henson's horses get their hooves checked and he catches up with trainer Jonjo O'Neill to see how his racehorses are coping with life off the track.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles recounts the story of a young woman who slipped and fell near the top of Snowdon in atrocious weather conditions, prompting Mountain Rescue to battle 100mph winds to save her. Plus, how paramedics fought to save the life of a cardiac patient whose heart stopped three times on the way to hospital.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: From their home bases in the New Forest, Cornwall, West Wales and the River Thames, Chris Packham, Gillian Burke, Iolo Williams and Steve Backshall share the latest stories from the wildlife cameras. Chris reveals how fish have more ways of making sound than any other vertebrate on the planet, Iolo discusses the intimate secrets of the bird families he has been following in his own back garden, and Michaela Strachan relives the dramatic story of a very skilful stoat in Minsmere in 2016. There is also the extraordinary spectacle of the firework sea anemone, and a rare reef made by beautiful peacock worms.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.title: The Home That Two Built
programme.desc: For the past 50 years, BBC Two programmes have been a guide to shifting lifestyle trends in contemporary Britain, reflected in home makeover, cookery and gardening shows. Mel Giedroyc narrates this documentary taking a nostalgic and light-hearted trawl through the archives to see how the channel has fared as tastemaker to the nation, with the first edition looking at the 1960s. Featuring contributions by Alan Titchmarsh, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Antony Worrall Thompson and Esther Rantzen.(n)

They also eat baked potatoes in roman ruins, a Chester tart next to the city walls, pasta in the Cathedral and are served a Civil War feast at one of the town's oldest pubs.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: England v Croatia. Action from the Euro 2004 Group B encounter at Estadio da Luz in Lisbon, where a young Wayne Rooney was in inspired form.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Highcliffe Castle, which was built from the ruins of several French great houses. Christina Trevanion and Adam Partridge pick out some gems.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his Edwardian Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo conducts important research in an historic tea room, built by an Edwardian immigrant to the city of York. In Leeds he discovers the city's textile heritage, and also investigates Bradford's musical heritage.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his tour of the solar system revealing that it was once home to not one, but two blue planets. Today, Mars is an arid desert world that long ago had the same potential as Earth. Which begs a big question - did life get a start on both worlds?(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a three-bed home in Surrey, which has a poorly positioned front door and a cluttered hallway. The owners are divided over whether an extension would solve their problems, and the architects present them with two very different visions for what their home could be. Laura takes a characteristically pragmatic approach with a simple but striking contemporary design. But experimental architect Robert decides a far more radical approach is the only answer.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the lives of 10 Guinea Street's occupants through the Second World War to the present day, discovering stories of love, loss and renewal. In the run-up to the war, the house is occupied by the younger generation of the Wallingtons, the long-term owners of number 10, who move back to the house after several years living in the rural Somerset. The Wallingtons are letting rooms to lodgers when war is declared in 1939, and would have sheltered in their cellar as German bombs rained down on Bristol. Over the 10 months of the Bristol Blitz, the ancient city is devastated and 3,000 houses destroyed. Number 10 itself is almost among them, as a bomb falls directly on Guinea Street, only missing the house by a whisker.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones, Scarlett Moffatt, Adil Ray, Angela Scanlon and Janet Street-Porter go out for dinner and play a series of games between courses, with the loser picking up the bill for the whole table.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Irwins experience 50 years of black British history, discovering how post-war Caribbean immigration transformed the way of life in the UK. The first edition sees them learn what life was like for arrivals coming to Britain in the 1950s and '60s, with presenter Giles Coren and social historian Emma Dabiri introducing them to their new homes and the family sampling the food, work and entertainment of first-generation West Indian immigrants in Brixton. With contributions by Kenny Lynch and Rudolph Walker.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the seven remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for reduce, reuse and recycle week. Joe Lycett kicks the group off with a pattern for a man's bomber jacket, which must be pieced together from four second-hand women's garments. In the transformation challenge, the sewers are tasked with changing laundry bags into a stylish and wearable garment, before finally being tasked to use old knitwear to make a made to measure jumper dress.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George comes up with a novel way of using old pots to create an easy rockery, while Brian builds a mini mountain feature with just stone and sand.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay demonstrates how to make a headboard, and there is a demonstration of how to build a brick barbecue for less than £60. Gavin Lowe has advice on wallpapering.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to make an offer on a collection of Matchbox toy cars, a collection of silver-plated goblets, a snuff box, and a model giraffe taken from a carousel.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: The team reviews the extraordinary events that have taken place during the week, as Gillian Burke looks back at her time with the Beaver family in Cornwall, Chris Packham reveals the extraordinary transformation young salmon perform and Iolo Williams reveals the latest findings about the mysterious migrations of Short Eared Owls. Plus, Steve Backshall introduces his young two-year-old son to a local family of swans.(n)

programme.title: The Home That Two Built
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc narrates this look at how BBC Two programmes kept track of lifestyle trends in the 1970s, an era of dodgy fashion and stodgy food that had people escaping into a world of stripped pine, country kitchens and Laura Ashley. This episode also shows that the decade had many similarities to today, from house price inflation and soaring energy costs through to worries about the threat to 'real' food and environmental sustainability. Featuring contributions by Eve Pollard, Gyles Brandreth, Wayne Hemingway, Loyd Grossman and Alan Titchmarsh.(n)

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their tour around Britain, following Samuel Chamberlain's 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. This leg takes them to Bristol, where they rub shoulders with Elizabethan pirates and learn about an ancient system of cultivation that uses fish waste as fertiliser. They also find time to cook up some gujarati street food, take a trip back to the 1970s and help decorate a chocolate version of the SS Great Britain.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Germany v Italy. A chance to see the Euro 2016 quarter-final at Nouveau Stade de Bordeaux in France.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon assess items at Margam Country Park in South Wales, while presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of one of his heroes, a Welshman who took Hollywood by storm.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to the steel city of Sheffield where he discovers an early 20th century steam engine which produced armour plates for a Royal Navy warship. Then, Michael heads south into Nottinghamshire and to the former pit village of Eastwood, before arriving in Nantwich, where he takes a dip in the briny.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines Jupiter, the largest and oldest planet in the solar system, revealing how its immense gravity influenced where other planets developed, destroying several nascent planets and negatively influencing the development of Mars. Today, Jupiter's great mass continues to be felt. Its gravity torments its moon Io, creating the most volcanically active body in the solar system. And through the vice-like grip it exerts over the asteroid belt, Jupiter's influence extends as far as Earth.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Britain Fed
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Ade Adepitan gain access to some of Britain's biggest supermarkets and their suppliers to see how they are dealing with the coronavirus outbreak, the most testing time in their history. Sara finds out what goes on in one supermarket over 24 hours, from opening to closing and all through the night, to see how they are coping during the crisis. Elsewhere, Ade finds out how the nations' farms, factories and depots have risen to the challenge to produce and supply our supermarkets with food in these exceptional circumstances.(n)

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: Josh's father is still in danger, and Lise must try to get there in time to save him. As the killer's motives become ever clearer, Cardinal and Delorme travel to Toronto to search his abandoned flat Crime thriller, based on the John Cardinal Mysteries series by Canadian author Giles Blunt, starring Billy Campbell.(n)

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: Cardinal and Delorme try to extract enough information from a suspect to get a lead on the killer, as he makes off into the depths of the forest with his last two chosen victims. Will they be able to stop him in time? Crime thriller, based on the John Cardinal Mysteries series by Canadian author GIles Blunt, starring Billy Campbell.(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel more than 120 miles north of the Arctic Circle into Swedish Lapland to work in Icehotel in the village of Jukkasjarvi. Using two-ton blocks of ice hacked from the nearby River Torne, Giles works with a team of international artists to create individually crafted rooms.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Irwins experience life in the 1970s, 1980s and 1990s, discovering how a new generation of black Britons made their way through three decades of British history, guided by presenter Giles Coren and social historian Emma Dabiri. They meet former West Indies cricket captain Clive Lloyd and learn about the 1976 tour of England in which he led his side to victory, before reflecting on the Brixton Riots of the 1980s, and hosting a typical 1989-style party with DJ and producer Jazzie B of Soul II Soul.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Documentary about traditional Mexican crafts and those preserving them, beginning by following the making of a loose-fitting tunic known as a huipil.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a purple night-sky scene featuring mountain overlooking a little falling river.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga explores the enduring relationship between Britain and people whose origins lie in Africa, looking back over significant people and events throughout history. He visits Cumbria, Eastbourne and Hereford to uncover the stories of generations of people with African descent in Britain, unearthing moments in history that have almost been forgotten. In Eastbourne, David comes face to face with the remains of an ancient, mysterious skeleton nicknamed Beachy Head Woman, with analysis revealing that she was of African descent yet raised in East Sussex.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. She begins by visiting places rarely seen by Western audiences to reveal how the Persian empire was born, the mysterious writing of its early people and how its powerful kings built a vast realm that became the envy of the ancient world.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary telling the story of a group of American men and women studying for college degrees while in prison for serious crimes. Shot over four years in maximum and medium security institutions in New York state, Lynn Novick's film tackles the failure to provide meaningful rehabilitation for more than two million Americans living behind bars.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Concluding part of the documentary about prisoners enrolled in the Bard Prison Initiative, featuring the background stories of participants and their families.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby studies the art and artefacts that chart England's increasing international influence under Henry VIII and his children, including the wreck of the Mary Rose and Holbein's The Ambassadors. He examines the power of religion with a visit to the Great Bible at Lambeth Palace, and reads the original copies of Shakespeare's history plays at the Globe Theatre.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Continuing his exploration of black British history, historian David Olusoga investigates further into the stories of thousands of Africans transported into slavery by the British, looking back at key historical events that turned Britain into the biggest slave-trading nation in the world, and shaped the future of Britain for black people. David learns about conflicting English laws that were implemented against the slaves and discovers more about the people who challenged these laws and contributed to the end of slavery.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga continue his exploration of black British history. Focusing on the Victorian era, David explores the moral crusade against slavery within this period of time and key moments that helped to shift the position of slavery within Britain. He discovers about mill workers of Rochdale that stood in solidarity with enslaved Africans in the American South, how Queen Victoria came to have a black god-daughter, and looks back at the victims of a tragedy in Jamaica.(n)

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga's exploration of the enduring relationship between Britain and people whose origins lie in Africa concludes. This time, he profiles the three African kings who took a stand against the machinations of an empire, along with a crooner whose voice was considered 'irresistible'. He also investigates a history of race riots in Liverpool, and outlines how the sense of a black British identity has been shaped by various forces throughout the span of the 20th century.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Following the process of the creation of a clay sculpture known as the Tree of Life, which was originally intended to teach bible stories to the indigenous people of Puebla.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape inspired by America's south west, featuring fresh skies above sun-dappled mountains and a silvery mere.(n)

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.sub-title: New Hidden Killers: The Victorian Home
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which historian Suzannah Lipscomb lays bare the dangers of domestic life in the 19th century, and reveals some of the harrowing consequences that resulted from urban growth, fashionable items and mass consumerism. She begins by examining how Victorian ladies surrounded themselves with highly dangerous new products, from the food they ate to the clothes they wore.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Sailors, Ships and Stevedores: The Story of British Docks
programme.desc: The transformation of British docks, many of which have changed from commercial portals to the world into clean, contemporary hubs for leisure and the arts. Narrated by Sue Johnston.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.desc: The documentary looks back at the moment Neil Armstrong climbed down from his lunar module and became the first man to walk on the moon, followed 15 minutes later by his crewmate Buzz Aldrin - a historic moment watched live by millions of viewers around the world and a nervous team back in the Nasa control centre.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Timeshift: How to Win at Chess
programme.desc: British grandmasters Dan King and Ray Keene offer tips on playing chess, taking viewers through a special demonstration game from opening gambit to checkmate and revealing the key moves that can lead to victory. They reveal how to outwit an opponent using techniques including forks, pins and skewers. Also taking party are novelist Martin Amis, writer Dominic Lawson, Britain's youngest grandmaster David Howell and Under-16 champion Sheila Dines.(n)

programme.title: Christo and Jeanne-Claude: Monumental Art
programme.desc: Documentary which charts the creation of world-renowned artist Christo's first public work of art in the UK, the Mastaba. In the summer of 2018, on the Serpentine in London's Hyde Park, Christo constructed the Mastaba from 7,506 painted oil barrels. The work was inspired by ancient Mesopotamian tombs and weighs six hundred tonnes. It is the latest work in a career spanning half a century and stretching across the world, and the programme looks back on Christo's career and a life spent making provocative works of art with his wife and partner Jeanne-Claude.(n)

programme.title: Looking for Rembrandt
programme.desc: Desperate for cash, the artist takes on commissions that his pupils have rejected, and looks for creative ways to divert money into his pocket as all of his possessions are taken by creditors. Rembrandt's work takes on a new style, embracing humanity rather than hubris - but this is not to everyone's taste.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape inspired by America's south west, featuring fresh skies above sun-dappled mountains and a silvery mere.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico

programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Following the creation of alebrijes, which are brightly coloured, fantastical creatures carved from copal wood and decorated in extremely detailed paintwork.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a secret little loft hidden in the woods, surrounded by evergreens and ruffled, green bushes and grass.(n)

programme.title: Shock and Awe: The Story of Electricity
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili explains how the study of electric fields and electromagnetic waves led to breakthroughs in communications and broadcasting, creating a world more dependent than ever on electrical power. He also investigates why scientists researching electrical superconductivity believe their work could have far-reaching effects.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp.(n)

programme.title: The Making of Spain
programme.sub-title: Blood and Gold: The Making of Spain with Simon Sebag Montefiore
programme.desc: The historian uncovers the truth about Spain's hero El Cid. He also investigates the attack on the city-state of Granada, waged by King Ferdinand and Queen Isabella, the horror of the Spanish Inquisition, and in the process discovers an unsettling story about one of his own ancestors.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You

programme.title: The Unshockable Dr Ronx
programme.desc: Pilot episode for a series in which A&E doctor Ronx Ikharia visits people reluctant to go to their GP, turning up in boxing gyms, nail salons and cafes or turning up in patients' homes. Ronx offers advice on mental health, excess sweating, fungal nails, and gender identity concerns, and comes to the aid of a trans man suffering pain from the binder flattening his chest and a woman who is going bald.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: An urgent call is made by a man whose wife is in cardiac arrest, with a doctor and paramedic team from North West Air Ambulance immediately dispatched to assist on the job. Back in control, a call is in progress for a patient suffering chest pains as a result of cocaine binge. Meanwhile out on the road, crewmates Chris and V are dispatched to a category-three call for a patient who's fallen. However, once the patient is safely treated the couple's dogs become the focus of the paramedics' attention. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby is devastated when Ian tells him that Peter kissed Dotty, while Linda hears how Phil has been treating Sharon and tells Mick she no longer wants to let him have the Vic. Chantelle tries to clear the air with Gray by proving she was at the dentist when he thought she was using his car - but he still thinks she is lying - and Sonia, feeling lonely, decides to search for her long-lost father.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: Tracy Daszkiewicz and Supt Dave Minty watch the bodycam footage of DS Bailey walking round the Skripal house and finally make a breakthrough, when they realise he and the Skripals were contaminated by Novichok placed on the front door of the house. As Nick fights for his life, his wife Sarah is beside herself with worry, but tries to remain calm for their daughters. Fact-based drama, starring Rafe Spall and Anne-Marie Duff.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: The action flashes back to three months before the incident as Terry visits Arabella in Ostia, a district of Rome on the Tyrrhenian Sea, where the up-and-coming author is having a whale of a time - all at the expense of her agent. After procuring supplies from local drug dealer Biagio, the pair have a wild night out that takes an unexpected turn for both of them. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Marouane Zotti.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer, alongside Maya Jama and Alex Horne, hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Tonight, singer-songwriter Liam Gallagher and comedian Rob Beckett join the show for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp and Kevin Pietersen go head to head in The Sporting Fix. Plus, an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions from James Bay.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Everton winger Theo Walcott, 2006 Commonwealth heptathlon champion Kelly Sotherton, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin and Welsh rugby great Scott Quinnell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda leaves Phil in absolutely no doubt that she doesn't want to sell him the pub, so Mick asks Sharon to convince her friend it's for the best. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian struggles to cope as a patient dredges up the past, threatening his relationship with Jac, who has uncovered something suspicious concerning Kestrel. Louis works with Guy on a case, only to fall out when he discovers he has been the subject of hospital gossip. Fletch awaits a confirmed date for his operation, only to cause chaos when he makes a false assumption concerning a garish gift.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: Charlie gives Dawn a bottle of perfume, unaware it contains more of the toxin. She collapses and is rushed to hospital within minutes of spraying herself with it. Nick is keen to get back to work but is still struggling with the trauma he went through, while Tracy's faith that the worst had passed is shattered as the team races to discover what else has been missed.(n)

Jay visits Ben, who is suffering with a fever, but he is shocked when his friend collapses.(n)

Fletch awaits a confirmed date for his operation, only to cause chaos when he makes a false assumption concerning a garish gift.(n)

programme.title: The Salisbury Poisonings
programme.desc: Charlie gives Dawn a bottle of perfume, unaware it contains more of the toxin. She collapses and is rushed to hospital within minutes of spraying herself with it. Nick is keen to get back to work but is still struggling with the trauma he went through, while Tracy's faith that the worst had passed is shattered as the team races to discover what else has been missed.(n)

programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the four remaining contestants take part in the semi final, creating garments inspired by silver screen. programme.sub-title: True North: Big Night at the Bingo
programme.desc: Documentary following the regulars at a West Belfast bingo hall, as they prepare for one of the busiest nights of the year - when the prize money rises to four figures. programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: David and Michael struggle to regain each other's trust, and a show producer has to step in to try and repair the damage. Comedy, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at some of the best moments from the Michael McIntyre's Big Show series, revisiting some of the biggest laughs from the past five seasons. Tonight, he looks back on the hilarious Send To All's from Doctor Who star Bradley Walsh and comedian Alan Carr. Chef Gino D'Acampo gets a rude awakening to play the Midnight Gameshow, and one audience member plays a game of Soap Or Nope - and receives her very own cliffhanger when she meets her pen-pal of 70 years for the very first time.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: The puffin family is woken through the night by a very strange noise which is revealed to be a frog who can't find his way to the pond after his hibernation. He needn't worry though as the puffins are on hand to help.(n)

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.sub-title: Pàdraig Post agus na Brògan Barraicht' Diet (Postman Pat and the Super Jet Boots)
programme.desc: Seonaidh awaits a special pair of shoes - jet boots that he plans to showcase at the Festival of Flight. However, the boots go an an unexpected detour before delivery.(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula and Bing are busy gathering acorns, but Bing's haul mysteriously disappears.(n)

programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald reads A' Bhana-phrionnsa agus a' Mhuc, by Jonathan Emmet, illustrated by Poly Bernatene and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod.(n)

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Through her artwork, Maria attempts to convey an important message about ocean pollution.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Science is brought to life with fun experiments that can be tried at home.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.sub-title: Tara an torc-fhiadhaich (Berta the Wild Boar)
programme.desc: Documentary about Berta, a feisty young wild boar growing up in the forests of Europe in a family group called a 'sounder'.(n)

programme.title: Sugh an Eorna (History of Whisky)
programme.desc: In the second of his six-part series celebrating Scotch through the centuries, John Carmichael tries the malt whiskies of his home, the Isle of Islay.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary looking at high and low sports, including deep sea diving and mountain climbing.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Gregor is in Skye to taste a great recipe using shop-bought puff pastry. In Lewis, an old friend shows him the secret to the perfect oatcakes accompanied by a delicious salmon pate.(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Artist Catrìona Black makes a pilgrimage to the world of Fr Allan Macdonald in South Uist and Eriskay. The aim is to make an exhibition and book of hand-made prints inspired by the weird, wonderful and beautifully subtle Gaelic words he collected in the late 19th century.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri is hostile towards Alasdair following his revelation in the city, but nothing can prepare her for the shock she gets when an old book, placed in an unusual location, leads her to discover another chilling secret. Life is a struggle for Tormod as he tries his hand at crofting, and Iain and his friend Anndra are left humiliated after their business pitch at the Trust.(n)

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell returns to the Millennium Forum in Derry to present an episode featuring Jimmy Buckley and his daughter Claudia. Country music fans will know Jimmy best for his solo career, as well as being one third of the Three Amigos. His daughter Claudia is currently working on completing her first solo album.(n)

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: A seagull and a goat get in the way when Stephen Smallberg, who is suffering writer's block, is inspired to put pen to paper.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Karen Matheson performs versions of Calum Sgaire and Cànan nan Gàidheal, with an international cast of musicians, and with Galician musicians Xabier Diaz and Guadi Galego.(n)

programme.desc: Poor Miguel has fleas and is being driven crazy by the constant itching.(n)

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: An unusual monster comes between Twigs and the new badge he is working hard to earn.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)

programme.sub-title: Aleesha an t-Ailigeutair Ameireaganach (Aleesha the American Alligator)
programme.desc: Interesting facts about Aleesha, a baby American alligator that is queen of its swamp and one of a clutch of 40 brothers and sisters.(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Things move on for the Lewis crofter as he tries to make a go of crofting full-time. The first lambs appear, and Sweeny sells his first eggs. However, there are some unwanted visitors in the hen house.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring inventions in the 1960s.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar visits Glasgow, where she draws a colour portrait of singer Joy Dunlop.(n)

programme.title: Pròis Tartan (Tartan Pride)
programme.desc: The history of the first-ever international football match, which took place in 1872, and saw a Scottish side face their English rivals, setting a emotional, cultural and historical precedent that endures through to the modern day.(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Artist Catrìona Black makes a pilgrimage to the world of Fr Allan Macdonald in South Uist and Eriskay. The aim is to make an exhibition and book of hand-made prints inspired by the weird, wonderful and beautifully subtle Gaelic words he collected in the late 19th century.(n)

programme.title: Orain Oir
programme.desc: Delving into the Gaelic music archive to pick out the best performances through the decades.(n)

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: Musical folk trio Lau perform one of their own tunes - Far from Portland.(n)

programme.title: Slighe Anndra
programme.desc: Exploring Scottish churches in cities across Europe. Featured locations include London, Lisbon, Paris and Rome.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.title: Bundesliga nam Ban
programme.desc: VfL Wolfsburg v SC Freiburg (Kick-off 1.00pm). Coverage of the women's German top-flight clash at AOK Stadion.(n)

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: A Canadian trio perform one of their tunes mixing traditional-folk with a trance-folk beat.(n)

programme.title: Two Go to Tolsta - Sgeulachd Syd Agus Millie
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Millie and Syd, two children from Hertfordshire who ran away to their grandparents' in 1949.(n)

programme.title: Am BFG (The BFG) (1989)
programme.desc: A friendly giant befriends a lonely little girl and transports her to a strange land. Unfortunately, a number of the other giants there have acquired a taste for eating children. Determined to stop their nefarious habit, the pair set out to charm the Queen of England into putting her armed forces at their disposal. Animated adventure, based on Roald Dahl's classic tale, with the voices of Donald Smith, Catriona Lexy Campbell and Donna Morrison.(n)

programme.title: Lon Le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod is joined by Hannah and Ailig to make fishcakes with potatoes and smoked salmon.(n)

programme.title: Peataichean/Pets
programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack meets Sophie and her guinea pigs, Timmy and Max.(n)

programme.title: Srath Sona (Happy Valley)
programme.desc: The children stay indoors to learn about the weather when it starts to rain, but Gwen prefers to be splashing in the puddles outside.(n)

programme.title: Na Floogals
programme.desc: The children are busy blowing bubbles, causing much excitement for the Floogals.(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing finds the shoes he had as a baby.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.sub-title: An Taigh Beag ri Taobh na Mara
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis reads An Taigh Beag ri Taobh na Mara, written and illustrated by Natalie Russell and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart.(n)

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall
programme.desc: Ag Ath-bheothachadh ar Seanair! When Jirka and Brona go to visit Grandad they are shocked to see how poorly he seems to be. However, while the grown-ups fret over Grandad's condition, the kids make a plan that will see him back on his feet. Short drama.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to turn a pile of sugar cubes into a skyscraper and win a water bomb competition. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.sub-title: Mireag am Mathan Dubh (Beth the Black Bear)
programme.desc: Documentary featuring facts about black bears. Beth is a cute bear cub, growing up with her brother under their mother's watchful eye. This is a story of action and adventure that includes eating, getting stuck in trees and taking a stroll in the nearest town. It's also a race for survival, as the little one will needs to have eaten enough to see her through her first hibernation.(n)

programme.title: Sugh an Eorna (History of Whisky)
programme.desc: In the second of his six-part series celebrating Scotch through the centuries, John Carmichael tries the malt whiskies of his home, the Isle of Islay.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary looking at high and low sports, including deep sea diving and mountain climbing.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Leugh Mi (Book Show)
programme.desc: Sandra Corbett from Lewis and Fanch Bihan-Gallic from Brittany discuss books of special interest to them. Presented by Anna Macleod.(n)

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell hosts the Irish-language country music show from the Millennium Forum in Derry, featuring Louise Morrisey, Frank McCaffrey, Paddy O'Brien and Mary Duff.(n)

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: Dallahan sing Jail Song, a traditional song with additional contemporary lyrics.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri strives to get her life back on track as Alasdair moves in with Iseabail. Sensing Alasdair's heartache, Iseabail takes the initiative to fix their relationship by telling Màiri the truth.(n)

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh learn about traditional music in Cork, accompanied by singer Mary Smith and fiddle-player Rachel Harris.(n)

programme.title: Bliadhna na Sgoile (My School Year)
programme.desc: Following a school year through the eyes of a teacher, a parent and pupils at the Gaelic unit at Gilcomstoun Primary School in Aberdeen. The bond between pupil and teacher is strong and there are plenty of tears and laughter on this emotional journey. Narrated by Patricia Macleod.(n)

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.(n)

They have an endless supply of beauty products, some beers, a microwave and a karaoke machine. With Paul the main topic of conversation, Mandy and Vinny navigate through their past and soon Mandy discloses a long-held secret that has affected her whole life. Vinny at last understands her resentments and fears.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla insists that she has no idea who Chelsey is, but is shocked when she reminds Ms Connor that she stayed in her squat when she was sleeping rough last year. Sally refuses to sit with Tim and Geoff in the pub, and Daniel is thrilled when Adam offers to spend his birthday with him and Bertie.(n)

programme.desc: Nina Hossain puts viewers' questions on a range of subjects relating to the Covid-19 outbreak to a panel of expert guests.(n)

programme.title: Grow Your Own at Home with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan harvests the first of the radishes he planted as seeds in the first edition of the programme and steps inside his greenhouse to grow an exotic treat or two. Frances Tophill gives a seed-to- plate guide to growing chillies, David Domoney grows barbecue favourites and Katie Rushworth gets healthy with her pick of the best super-foods to grow in even the tiniest of spaces.(n)

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: The detectives have their work cut out identifying key suspects, especially as the location of Danny's murder is still a mystery, but Miller discovers some suspicious items taped under the victim's bed and in his sister Chloe's room, which could help them get closer to the truth. Meanwhile, Hardy finds an isolated clifftop hut that arouses his curiosity, the Latimers struggle to cope with their grief and the Rev Paul Coates decides to address the local media. Crime drama, starring David Tennant, Olivia Colman, Arthur Darvill, Andrew Buchan and Jodie Whittaker.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin search for a nursery now that Janice has left, and Mrs Tishell is shocked to find out that Al has very high blood pressure. Louisa also helps with the diagnosis of a pupil, which spurs her on to explore the possibilities of a change of career. This is not welcome news for Martin, who dislikes any kind of change, even if it could be for the better. Elsewhere, Ruth is approached about selling her farm. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz, Selina Cadell and Ian McNeice.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts six more contestants through their paces to see who will have the opportunity to answer 15 questions to win the prize of £1,000,000. Each player has four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders.(n)

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the dramatic story of an amateur pilot who built his own plane, but then lost his propeller mid-flight and had to try to land it without any power. A local resident in northern California tries to flee what goes down in history as the state's deadliest wildfire, and a cliff collapse at a popular tourist spot in Greece causes chaos.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special to begin the second series, the actor and film-maker examines the real-life impact of dementia. He meets Scott Mitchell, the husband of his former on-screen mum Barbara Windsor, who was diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease in 2014. Ross also travels to Leatherhead, Surrey, where he stays with Jerry Beckman, an actor, magician and keen sportsman who has early-onset dementia and Parkinson's disease.(n)

programme.title: Culinary Genius
programme.desc: Phil Vickery, husband of host Fern Britton, acts as judge as nine home cooks are whittled down in three intense culinary battles. Phil starts by demonstrating how to finely puree garlic before contestants must attempt their own in just five minutes. Then six players are tasked to prepare a loin of venison from a rack, before the last three are given just two minutes to select from the larder containing an array of fresh fruit and vegetables, pulses and grains, condiments and sauce ingredients to pare with their prepared venison. A prize of £1,000 awaits the successful candidate.(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: A special series looking back at Bradley and Barney's previous American adventures. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders.(n)

Phil starts by demonstrating how to finely puree garlic before contestants must attempt their own in just five minutes. Then six players are tasked to prepare a loin of venison from a rack, before the last three are given just two minutes to select from the larder containing an array of fresh fruit and vegetables, pulses and grains, condiments and sauce ingredients to pare with their prepared venison. A prize of £1,000 awaits the successful candidate.(n)

DCI Barnaby's investigation unearths more than mere local hostility as he lifts the lid on long-buried secrets. Anna Calder-Marshall and Margaret Tyzack guest star.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mike gets involved in the troubles of a newcomer and her unruly daughter, only to learn her dark past is about to catch up with her in the form of a ruthless reporter. Meanwhile, Vernon decides there is money to be made from goat's milk. Jason Durr stars.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tempers fray as a kind gesture by Isobel backfires, while Mary fights Branson's corner when his decisions are put under scrutiny. Robert and Cora's relationship is under pressure, but can Violet make them see things differently? Bates goes to extreme lengths as his chance of freedom slips away. Costume drama, starring Penelope Wilton, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an RAF officer is found hanged in a wood, Meredith - the new chief superintendent at Hastings - is quick to dismiss it as a straightforward suicide. However, an aerial photo of a German village found in the dead man's pocket leads Milner to suspect murder. The newly retired Foyle is compelled to return to service when events take an even more unexpected turn. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry receives the final papers for his divorce. An environmental health inspector inspects the Munchbox. Jack and Biff search for Robert when he goes missing.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert goes to get help leaving Andy stuck at the quarry. Terry makes advances towards Tina. Sean informs a stunned Dave that he has set him up with Mandy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is furious to find Jack behind the bar with buxom Lorraine. As he prepares to go to the Palace, Alf feels he is unworthy to receive the OBE.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf and Audrey leave for Buckingham Palace, and he humiliates her when she is refused entry. Vicky is annoyed to discover Fiona has cut Steve's hair.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nicholson delays his 21st-birthday celebrations to join Bellamy in pursuit of two armed and dangerous criminals, but takes one risk too many. Meanwhile, Vernon finds himself blowing his own trumpet after hiring out a brass band to Lord Ashfordly. Rural police drama, starring Ryan Early and Mark Jordon.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: It's a happy day for Downton as Anna's patience is rewarded, while Edith receives admiration from an unexpected quarter. Matthew refuses to back down over the estate management, but will his tactics pay off? Branson's plan for the christening causes consternation, as does the arrival of his brother, and O'Brien's motives are unclear as she counsels Thomas. Julian Fellowes' period drama, starring Joanne Froggatt, Laura Carmichael, Dan Stevens and Allen Leech.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg tells Eric that he won't be taking the job for personal reasons. Liz is upset by the way she and Jim keep annoying each other. Kevin gets a new assistant at the garage.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des is disturbed by his feelings for Liz. Kevin is infuriated by Barry and his passion for punctuality. Jim cooks Liz a special meal to show her he loves her.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris attempts to find out why Kim is back at Home Farm. Susie plans a charity night for the Glovers. Zak and Butch steal a freezer, but there's a shock waiting for them inside it.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It's Roy's birthday and he announces that he's left school. Frank is determined that Kim will never be allowed to forget what she has done to him.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor is suspected of involvement in the theft of a priceless Ming vase. Mike tries to discover the reason for Merton's visit to headquarters and Gina starts acting like Aidensfield's answer to Mata Hari. Nostalgic drama, guest starring Leslie Grantham.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's jealousy rears its ugly head, but Mildred is in no hurry to put his mind at rest.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's brother discovers documents that prove Mildred married an Army deserter. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's desire to play the guitar proves too much for his flatmates. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man who has not been heard of in 20 years and was presumed dead suddenly makes an appearance in Aidensfield - and seems not to have aged a day - prompting Merton to cross swords with an old adversary. Meanwhile, Vernon's attempts to bring a taste of France to North Yorkshire are swiftly ruined as a wine connoisseur dishes the dirt on Scripps' vineyards.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is confused to find her missing freezer has been mysteriously returned. Kim tries to tell Dave she had no choice but to leave him. Marcus and his friends threaten Scott.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank insists Kim accompanies him to the charity night at the Woolpack, and he taunts Dave at the bar. Terry tries to tell Alan about Jan stealing.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells Kevin to find a mechanic or take Barry back. Reg discovers that Eric is going to offer the job to the manager of his Halifax branch and races there to head him off.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf is happy to help Maud out at the shop as Maureen settles Reg in Lowestoft. Sean questions Des and Liz over their relationship. Jack phones an agency to try out new barmaids.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Guest starring Timothy West and Hannah Gordon, along with an early appearance by a young Orlando Bloom as the first victim.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man who has not been heard of in 20 years and was presumed dead suddenly makes an appearance in Aidensfield - and seems not to have aged a day - prompting Merton to cross swords with an old adversary. Meanwhile, Vernon's attempts to bring a taste of France to North Yorkshire are swiftly ruined as a wine connoisseur dishes the dirt on Scripps' vineyards.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An exciting offer for Edith divides opinion, Isobel throws Ethel a lifeline and Anna's perseverance finally pays off. Daisy is unhappy when Alfred takes a keen interest in newly arrived maid Ivy, while Matthew's concerns about the estate grow - but are his protests falling on deaf ears? Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Penelope Wilton, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Dan Stevens.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a woman's disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth.(n)

programme.desc: A valuable heirloom brought back from the China Sea could change the Ropers' fortunes - but not if Mildred's sister gets it first. Starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The pathologist investigates the death of a child with a rare disease, and a new colleague of Quincy working in the lab believes the child was murdered by its father.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo investigates a series of murders of staff at a psychiatric hospital. A doctor is convinced one of her patients is the killer, but Theo unearths another possibility.(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Mycroft Holmes joins his brother to investigate the murder of a professor's secretary. The only clue is a golden pince-nez found clenched in the hand of the victim. Charles Gray guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur purchases a consignment of satellite dishes, enlisting the aid of a former television repair man to fit them. However, his contractor proves to be afraid of heights. Meanwhile, Ray develops an interest in the daughter of volatile Italian businessman 'Fingers' Rosetti. Pete Postlethwaite guest stars, with George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is approached by a woman desperate to prove her imprisoned father is innocent of murder. As he proceeds, the case causes him to become plagued by doubt and believe he sent an innocent man to jail for the murder of his former partner. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is assigned to ensure the safe shipment of a gold bullion consignment, threatened by the plans of a brilliant computer expert bent on revenge. Drama, guest starring George Cole and John Hurt, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.sub-title: May the Horse Be with You
programme.desc: A retired cop who has been secretly housing racehorses in the NYPD stables is injured when he tries to prevent the theft of one of his valuable animals. Theo sets out to track down the culprit as the police are made aware of the situation. Detective drama, starring Telly Savalas.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A paparazzi photographer becomes the prime suspect in a jewel theft and the phony kidnapping of a former actress, and helps Theo to bring the real criminals to justice.(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A reclusive lodger's strange behaviour sends his landlady running to Holmes for advice - and what initially seems to be a simple matter turns into a mystery involving an Italian secret society and murder in New York. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A business deal backfires badly, leaving the future of the Winchester in doubt, so Ray organises a fundraising football match against the police. However, with kick-off looming, one of Arthur's incompetent associates appears with a huge sum of money stolen from a rival businessman, leaving the the dodgy dealer seeking a way to stash the cash without it being noticed. George Cole and Gary Webster star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A stakeout in a bowling alley escalates into a full-scale alert when an atom bomb is discovered, primed and ready to detonate. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star in an instalment featuring a fleeting early appearance by Pamela Stephenson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A new recruit cannot cope with the stresses of the job and puts the lives of other squad members in jeopardy. A young Lesley Dunlop (Emmerdale's Brenda Walker) features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Fern Britton, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard, lingerie tycoon Michelle Mone and actor Neil Morrissey pit their general knowledge against a trivia expert as they try to win thousands of pounds for charity. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The island of Andros in the West Indies boasts tales of swimmers being ripped from the surface of the island's mysterious blue holes by a monster known as the Lusca. Described as half-shark, half-octopus with a taste for human flesh, it is said to drag its prey a thousand feet down to its deep water lair. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the tropical paradise, with his quest for answers taking him to dangerous new depths and closer than ever to a real killer.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Team Bold Contender head to the formidable Pedra Branca. The location is famous for its monster waves and not many lobstermen hunt there because the swell is just too rough.(n)

programme.title: Cop Land (1997)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon

Meanwhile, Murtaugh finds a joint stashed in his kitchen that he brings to Scorsese for testing in an effort to bust one of his children. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The island of Andros in the West Indies boasts tales of swimmers being ripped from the surface of the island's mysterious blue holes by a monster known as the Lusca. Described as half-shark, half-octopus with a taste for human flesh, it is said to drag its prey a thousand feet down to its deep water lair. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the tropical paradise, with his quest for answers taking him to dangerous new depths and closer than ever to a real killer.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A 600-mile journey through one of the most world's most northern countries - Finland - from Helsinki in the south to Kemijärvi in Finnish Lapland. The line whisks passengers through the thickest of forests, past vast frozen lakes 63 times the size of Lake Windermere and to station stops where time has stood still. As the train enters the Arctic Circle, it calls at Rovaniemi, a place destroyed by German soldiers at the end of the Second World War. Now the town has been re-built, famously in the shape of a reindeer, serving as the official home of Santa Claus. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell tells the story behind the design and construction of Isambard Kingdom Brunel's famous bridge over the picturesque Avon gorge at Bristol, and discovers that some people question whether it was entirely the product of his own devising. The bridge remained unfinished in Brunel's lifetime, but when he died in 1859, his friends decided that the bridge should be completed as a memorial, using the chains from another of his suspension bridges that was being demolished.(n)

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow examines the life and legacy of German painter Albrecht Durer, who set a new benchmark in self-portraiture and raised the artistic reputation of northern Europe to a level rivaling that of Renaissance Italy. Like his contemporary Leonardo da Vinci, Durer perceived the world with a scientist's eye, and his detailed studies of plants and animals remain unrivalled to this day.(n)
