programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly for Help the Animals
programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall helps famous faces solve their dogs' behavioural problems, using his experience to find quick but long-lasting solutions. Graeme visits Essex, where actress and presenter Denise Van Outen is being given the runaround by her French bulldog Tilly. He then meets opera singer Russell Watson, whose saluki Poppy has a fear of other dogs, before helping former England striker Michael Owen, whose Staffordshire bull terrier Ronnie has attacked dogs in the past.(n)

programme.title: This Week on the Farm
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Jules Hudson present a show in which farmers document their lives. The first episode comes from Cannon Hall Farm in South Yorkshire, and features a Highland cow getting ready for an online country show, the construction of a new home for a herd of sheep, and a vet treating a lamb with a broken leg. In Carlisle, a herd of cattle are let loose on the Cumbrian salt marshes, and in North Yorkshire, a financial advisor becomes a vegetable picker.(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle reunites with a former medical student living in the woodlands of Arkansas. Ben discovers the impact a series of injuries have had on his physically demanding wild lifestyle, including a simple lapse in concentration that nearly cost him everything. However, none of this has prevented him honing his talents as a craftsman and embracing a life surrounded by nature.(n)

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: The psychology of Charles Manson, an American cult leader whose followers murdered seven people in their California homes over two nights in August 1969. Detectives, lawyers and psychiatrists analyse what prompted the crimes, and examine how Manson was able to persuade his acolytes to take the lives of innocent people.(n)

programme.title: Murder in Mind: The Manson Killings (2016)
programme.desc: The story of Linda Kasabian, who during the drug-fuelled summer of 1969 fell prey to the hypnotic charms of murderous criminal Charles Manson and his self-proclaimed 'family' of followers. Fact-based thriller, starring Mackenzie Mauzy, starring Mackenzie Mauzy, Eden Brolin and Jeff Ward.(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell offers an insight into the history, design and building process of the Britannia Bridge, across the Menai Strait between the island of Anglesey and the mainland of Wales. The difficult task of building the 100ft iron structure was taken up by civil engineer Robert Stephenson and his team, including William Fairbairn and Eaton Hodgkinson. But in May 1970, four youths set light to the famous bridge, and Rob meets a fireman who fought the blaze that fateful night.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels from the west of Morocco to the east, heading into the desert on a mission to find out whether a railway line once crossed the Sahara all the way to Timbuktu in Mali. He begins in Marrakesh, taking in Casablanca and Fez before visiting a town that he is convinced has been named in his honour.(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An insight into the working life of officers patrolling the nation's roads, using modern technology and old-fashioned methods to bring criminals to justice. Presented by Jamie Theakston. After an hour-long chase the team use a stinger to stop a motorbike. In Harrogate, a hostile crowd gathers when a suspect runs into a housing estate.(n)

programme.title: The Railway 24/7
programme.desc: The network is severely disrupted by the 80mph winds of Storm Ciara, leaving lines blocked by fallen trees and stranded passengers in need of a new route home. British transport police officers Tom and Chris take on a demanding weekend night shift, and have to deal with a group of young men who refuse to leave Stockport station. Conductor Jess dreads the arrival of a crowd of football fans - but they turn out to be a much more welcome arrival than expected.(n)

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A man who fell through a window has a phobia of needles, which leads him to refuse any kind of pain relief and even attempt to discharge himself before his injuries are treated. An aggressive patient is brought in by four police officers after smashing up his mother's home, and has to undergo a toxicology test. Dr Audrey Fox tries to save the life of a patient who has overdosed, and a woman suffers a head injury after slipping on a slice of pizza her husband had left on the floor for their dog to eat.(n)

programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. A team of one hundred officers prepare to strike with a series of simultaneous dawn raids, and police in south Wales identify a target couriering drugs around the UK.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: A young deaf girl learns sign language to try and find herself, but instead she becomes caught between the deaf and hearing worlds.(n)

programme.title: 49th Parallel (1941)
programme.desc: Six Nazis are left stranded in Canada after their U-boat is sunk. As they try to escape the attentions of the authorities, they struggle to win sympathy among the locals. But when that fails, they decide to flee to the still-neutral USA instead. Michael Powell's Oscar-winning Second World War thriller, starring Anton Walbrook, Leslie Howard, Laurence Olivier, Glynis Johns and Niall MacGinnis.(n)

programme.title: Samson and Delilah (1949)
programme.desc: Hebrew warrior Samson becomes a wanted man after crossing paths with Philistine troops, but he constantly thwarts their efforts to capture him using to his incredible strength. Determined to bring him down, the Saran of Gaza makes use of his beguiling sister Delilah, who seduces Samson and manages to rob him of the source of his power. Cecil B DeMille's Oscar-winning adaptation of the biblical tale, starring Victor Mature, Hedy Lamarr, George Sanders, Angela Lansbury and Henry Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Donovan's Reef (1963)
programme.desc: An American soldier settles down with a friend and a kindly doctor on a South Sea island to relax in the aftermath of the Second World War. The arrival of the medic's snobbish daughter throws their idyllic life into turmoil, leaving them struggling to make her father seem respectable. Comedy drama directed by John Ford, starring John Wayne, Lee Marvin, Jack Warden, Elizabeth Allen and Cesar Romero.(n)

programme.title: X-Men 2 (2003)
programme.desc: Mutant heroes the X-Men are forced into an uneasy alliance with arch-enemy Magneto to battle a common foe. Following an attempt on the US president's life, a sinister military scientist launches a raid on Professor Xavier's school and hatches a plan to rid the world of all mutants. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Hugh Jackman, Patrick Stewart, Ian McKellen, Alan Cumming, Halle Berry and Brian Cox.(n)

programme.title: Logan (2017)
programme.desc: In the near future, Wolverine cares for an ailing Professor X somewhere on the Mexican border. However, the razor-clawed hero's attempts to hide from the world are turned upside down when a young mutant arrives, pursued by lethal forces. Fantasy adventure, starring Hugh Jackman, Patrick Stewart, Dafne Keen and Stephen Merchant.(n)

programme.title: Stoker (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager living an isolated life in her mother's remote estate discovers she has an uncle she has never met following her father's death. The mysterious relative moves into the house and seems to take over his brother's role in the family. His niece suspects he has sinister motives, but becomes dangerously infatuated with him. Thriller, starring Mia Wasikowska, Nicole Kidman and Matthew Goode.(n)

programme.title: The Brand New Testament (2015)
programme.desc: God secretly lives in Brussels, spending his time enacting spiteful punishments on the human race. His rebellious daughter tries to teach him a lesson by sending everyone on Earth a message containing the date of their death. As the world is plunged into chaos, she flees from her furious father - who gains a new insight into his creation. Comedy, starring Pili Groyne, Benoit Poelvoorde and Catherine Deneuve. In French and German.(n)

programme.title: The Gunfight at Dodge City (1959)
programme.desc: A gambler becomes the sheriff of a frontier town, planning to use his position to avenge his brother's murderers - but the arrival of a face from the past muddies the water, and leaves him doubting the identity of the killer. Western, starring Joel McCrea, Julie Adams, John McIntire, Nancy Gates and Richard Anderson.(n)

programme.title: The Miracle of Morgan's Creek (1943)

programme.title: The Killing (1956)

programme.title: Shane (1953)
programme.desc: A mysterious gunfighter intervenes when a man and his family are threatened by a predatory cattle baron determined to force every homesteader in the valley off the land. The situation rapidly escalates into violence, while an unspoken attraction develops between the gunman and the settler's wife. Western, starring Alan Ladd, Jack Palance, Van Heflin and Jean Arthur. Based on the novel by Jack Schaefer.(n)

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II (1988)
programme.desc: The rugged Australian adventurer sets out to save his reporter girlfriend from New York drug dealers. Having rescued her from their clutches, he realises they will soon be coming after him - so he decides to even the odds by luring them to the Outback for a final showdown. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Paul Hogan, Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Charles S Dutton.(n)

Action thriller sequel based on Lee Child's novel, starring Tom Cruise, Cobie Smulders and Danika Yarosh.(n)

programme.title: Patti Cake$ (2017)

programme.desc: Writer Mark has survived polio, but still needs to use an iron lung. At the age of 38, he has begun a writing career but has never had sex. With the support of his parish priest, he enlists the help of a sex therapist in gaining the confidence to lose his virginity. Comedy drama, starring John Hawkes, Helen Hunt and William H Macy.(n)

Western, starring John Wayne, Robert Mitchum, James Caan and Ed Asner.(n)

Romantic comedy, starring Cary Grant and Sophia Loren.(n)

Thriller, based on Frederick Forsyth's novel, starring Jon Voight, Derek Jacobi and siblings Maria and Maximilian Schell.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is excited to work a case with the best detective in the NYPD, Dave Majors - until his colleague shares his intentions to ask Amy out on a date. Rosa's surprising fashion choice causes a stir, and Charles and Gina make an unexpected discovery. Comedy, with Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon goes to Las Vegas and does everything he can to win a large sum of money for science. Meanwhile, Penny and Bernadette take Amy shopping for a wedding dress, but her terrible choice entangles them in a web of lies.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel finds out that Joe's ex-wife, Tiffany, has been keeping a big secret from him after the former lovers rekindle a romantic spark. Elsewhere, Lennox works on winning a popularity contest at school. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel's personal trainer immediately hits it off with Joe, while Lennox struggles with a crush of her own when she tries to stay just friends with Beckett. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben takes Emma to his old doctor for a check-up and admits he hasn't had a medical for years. programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley realises that she hasn't met any of Ross's friends and decides to throw a cocktail party so she can get acquainted with them. Eddie Cibrian guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amy work together on a case, but wind up arguing over Jake's old, worn-out mattress. Meanwhile, Boyle scratches Holt's prized car, and the teenage boy Rosa sponsors as a 'big brother' is in trouble with the law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is left looking after Terry's pregnant wife, only for panic to set in when her waters break sooner than expected. A computer outage forces Amy and Charles to process all the paperwork by hand. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.desc: Adam and Barry decide to write a song like the iconic Parents Just Don't Understand. Beverly keeps calling Erica at college, but Erica wants some space from her mother and screens her phone calls, forcing Beverly to adopt drastic measure.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole refurbishes a classic Ford Mustang and a neon garage sign, while Simon O'Brien restores a classic Chopper bicycle and turns a bass guitar into a chair.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets people as they bid to turn an empty plot of land into their dream home on an affordable budget. Adam and Vicki Nicholl have become tired of city life and decide to move back to the Hertfordshire village of Pirton where they grew up to build a barn-style property in the garden of Adam's mum's home. The pair are convinced they can save around £100,000 simply by not purchasing an existing property.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Royal Forester at Bewdley in Worcestershire for their second stay, with proprietors Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker hoping to impress their guests with a trip to a local safari park. Sarah and Luke are impressed with their colourful room, but Sanjeev and Elinor have a 'hairy' experience.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From Ferry Lodge in Holyhead, Anglesey, where mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway hope to impress the other contestants. However, things do not get off to a good start when Geri and Lorraine are overwhelmed by the number of laminated signs, Sean and Maxine begrudge paying 50p for toiletries and Sanjeev and Elinor are left unimpressed by the cleanliness of the establishment.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Partners Sanjeev Bhagotra and Elinor Roberts welcome their guests to Court Colman Manor, Bridgend, Glamorgan. Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are pleased with the size of their room on arrival, but Sarah Allaway discovers a collection of cobwebs in hers. During the afternoon, the groups take a lesson in fire-making, and the next morning a traditional Welsh breakfast is not to everyone's taste - with the hosts wondering whether tactical voting has played a part come feedback time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one last time to find out what they have been paid. Geri kicks off proceedings by demanding to know why Sarah and Luke did not want a return visit to Greenways of Malvern, before they in turn confront Sanjeev and Elinor over their low cleanliness score for Ferry Lodge. Then the Court Colman Manor proprietors accuse Sarah and Luke of unfairly judging their hotel, before the payments are revealed and the winners are announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Surrey and Hampshire. Simon builds a huge kitchen table from discarded rafters and gives a 1970s campervan a new lease of life. Henry hankers after an American tractor and Royal Enfield motorbike and sidecar.(n)

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the most difficult cases he has taken on since the series began. Next, the chef goes back to Chiarella's in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. When he first visited owners Tommy and Dina DeFino, the business was struggling so badly that Tommy had completely lost his confidence and wife Dina had lost her patience. The guests take part in an activity at a local cookery school where they are taught the art of preparing and cooking sheep's testicles. However, come feedback time, the hosts are left feeling distraught.(n)

programme.desc: The owners meet to find out who earned the most and have it out with their rivals. Mike and Charlie of the Valley Hotel want to get to the bottom of an unwanted intruder during their stay at Wisteria House, and the ladies are challenged again regarding their cleanliness comments. Emotions run high as the cash is counted and the winner is revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Grand Designs

Robert has designed his own heating system to achieve maximum sustainability, but the project begins with only £100,000 of the £400,000 budget in place, and he and his family have to move into the existing building while work goes on around them.(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Lost Pyramid
programme.desc: Documentary following archaeologists working on a 4,000-year-old pyramid containing a burial chamber, still apparently sealed. As light enters the tomb for the first time, the artefacts contained within reveal a gripping detective story as although there are no signs of entry, the contents have been disturbed. To solve the mystery, Egyptologists explore the nearby Black Pyramid to see how pyramid builders used every trick in the book to protect the treasures, discovering a dark and complex labyrinth of dummy tombs, false passages and stone portcullises to fool robbers.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cyclist Stephen, who's 48, has severely damaged his ankle after coming off his bike deep in woodland in Surrey, and doctors treat a man rushed in after collapsing with a suspected stroke in his garden. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gary, 50, is brought into St George's hospital by ambulance after a garage collapsed and fell on his legs. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In a first for the show, James May shocks the rest of the team when he puts his foot firmly on the accelerator of a Bugatti Veyron. Meanwhile, a case of bad driving and appalling trousers results in all three presenters getting thrown off a golf course. Actor Hugh Grant is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines the social significance of cars in the 20th century. He begins by travelling to Germany, Italy and Russia to examine how dictators kick-started the mobilization of the masses, finding tales of design brilliance, abject failure, war, fraud and double dealing. James also learns about how the British motor industry blew a gift-wrapped chance to rule the world and he gets his own back with a stunt that means bad news for one of the planet's most hated cars.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May work on a project to build mobility scooters capable of tackling the British countryside's terrain. Ferrari's four-wheel-drive FF is pitted against the revised, V8-engined Bentley Continental GT on a test track at the edge of the Arctic Circle, while James puts the Fisker Karma through its paces in Florida, and meets AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. James focuses on passenger comfort, Richard tries to shorten response times by getting other drivers out of the way more effectively, an issue Jeremy tackles with the invention of what he calls the 'sports ambulance'. Their three very different creations are then put to the test with a series of severe and often messy challenges. Plus, Formula One driver Daniel Ricciardo is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An exploration of the survival techniques used by creatures inhabiting the deep oceans of the world, where adaptation is paramount for those dwelling on the surface as well as below. Pods of dolphins are filmed charging in search of prey, surprising methods for conserving energy are revealed as deep-sea octopuses 'fly' with wings, and vampire squid use bioluminescence to create a colourful display.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: TV and radio presenter Chris Tarrant goes on three journeys by rail through some of the world's harshest terrain. He begins by heading to the Congo in central Africa to experience one of the greatest surviving railways of the colonial era, built by the French at a cost of tens of thousands of native lives and connecting the capital Brazzaville to the coast. Chris sets off on his scheduled two-day trip from the station at the port of Pointe-Noire, ending his journey six days - and a whole series of setbacks - later.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet

Judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finde will assess their efforts before deciding which three teams will be continuing to next week's round.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of well-known faces including Denise Van Outen, Nick Grimshaw, Joe Swash, Stacey Solomon, Mo Gilligan, Nicola Adams and Zoe Ball appraise Little Fires Everywhere, The Titan Games, Casino Royale, World Cup Final '66: England v West Germany, Naked Attraction and Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the popular words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Claudia Winkleman take on Jon Richardson and Henning Wehn. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Nick Helm to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls UK
programme.desc: A chilling call from an online predator in Essex leads to a confession of brutal murder, and in north London, emergency services respond to a horrifying incident after a van deliberately driven into a crowd outside a mosque. Elsewhere, a harrowing call is taken in the aftermath of a frenzied acid attack by a woman on her partner in Bristol.(n)

programme.title: Murder in the Car Park
programme.desc: Docudrama about the killing of private investigator Daniel Morgan, who was found in the car park of a south London pub with an axe lodged in his skull and £1,000 left poking out of his trouser pocket. The subsequent inquiry has lasted 33 years, and cost £30 million, but has failed to convict anyone of Morgan's murder. For the first time, those that were alleged to have committed the crime, the police officers who investigated, and the grieving brother of the victim all tell their story.(n)

programme.title: The Queen and the Coup
programme.desc: It's February 1953, the first anniversary of Queen Elizabeth's reign. Little does the Queen know she is about to be deployed in a US plot to topple Iran's democratic leader in favour of an all-powerful shah. Planned by MI6, and executed by the CIA, the coup destroyed Iran's democracy and had an impact on relations between Iran and the West. Using declassified secret documents, this documentary reveals the truth of what happened in 1953.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Big Night Out
programme.desc: The story of the night when the future Queen Elizabeth II slipped out of Buckingham Palace with Princess Margaret to party in secret on the streets of London. It was May 8, 1945 - VE Day - the end of the Second World War in Europe. Seventy years later, eyewitnesses who were with the princesses that evening, including the Queen's cousin and close friend Margaret Rhodes and lady-in-waiting Jean Woodroffe, reveal in detail what happened as the pair joined thousands of revellers celebrating peace.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following two families who have set up shop in Spain selling properties to fellow Brits looking for a home in the sun. Sharon Garner and her son Shaun help cleaning company owners Kevin and Kate Waring from Coventry find a villa in Almeria, while Sophie and Charlotte Rodriguez help Karen and Steve Cummins find their ideal holiday home in Alicante.(n)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: A vet has to dehorn a mother rhino and its calf as well as moving three males to a new home. Zoologists compare glucose levels between elephants in the wild and kept in zoos.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman goes house hunting on Spain's Costa Blanca with Barnsley couple Paul and Lisa, who cannot wait to buy their first property together in the ever-popular resort of Torrevieja. They have three children and seven grandchildren wanting to visit, so the pressure is on Jasmine to find a holiday home the whole family can enjoy. With a £72,000 budget, she shows them five options - but will any measure up to their expectations?(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw travel to Kent to pick up a very tired 1991 VW T4 Campervan. The vehicle's owner has recently had cancer of the brain and his wife is hoping the boys can help bring a ray of light to his life.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Ye Old Crown in Edenbridge, Kent, where chef and owner Aaron Webster aims to deliver a three star service for a two star price.(n)

programme.desc: Mother-of-three Donna Harris hosts the second dinner party in Northampton, and hopes that three courses of pure comfort-food will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. Donna spends almost as much time fixing her make-up as she does preparing the meal, and when guests Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash discover a secret stash of football shirts, they realise there is more to their hostess than meets the eye.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete for the cash prize of £1,000 in Bournemouth, beginning with Nikki and Steve, followed by accountant Peter and retired air stewardess Lyn, then Nicki and Emily.(n)

programme.desc: In the fifth season premiere, Molly returns home after eight weeks at the prestigious writers' workshop with exciting news - she is getting her book published and has received a generous advance. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.desc: Carl has a suggestion for Mike that regular sex could be stifling Molly's creativity, as she reluctantly admits that she is suffering from writer's block. Starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Amy (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary portrait of soul, jazz and R'n'B singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, whose untimely death in July 2011 followed a well-publicised history of drugs and alcohol abuse. Director Asif Kapadia traces Amy's rise and demise over the 13 years preceding her death, telling her story via her music and autobiographical song lyrics, video footage shot by friends and family, archive television clips, plus voiceover interviews with those who were personally and professionally close to her. Contributors include Pete Doherty, Mitch Winehouse, Tony Bennett, Mark Ronson and Tyler James.(n)

He was found to be unfit to plead and committed to Broadmoor Hospital, but during a brief escape from the facility in 1952 he killed again. It was determined that the cause of his behaviour was severe brain damage, traced back to an attack of encephalitis as a child in India. When he died in 2007, he was the longest-serving prisoner in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Jimmy Carr and the Science of Laughter - A Horizon Special
programme.desc: The comedian takes over the science programme in a special episode for BBC's sitcom season, turning it into a chat show with eminent scientists as guests. He talks to them about the nature of laughter, with the studio audiences serving as guinea pigs in his attempt to get to the bottom of what laughter is, what it has to do with comedy, and why human beings find it a pleasurable sensation. The programme reveals that laughter predates the human species and may have played a vital role in our evolution.(n)

Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Anton Yelchin and Irene Jacob.(n)

programme.title: Hallows Eve (2016)
programme.desc: Shadowy figures in masks begin appearing around an estate with a reputation for street crime, and leave behind graffiti warning of their intent to take over the area on Halloween. The members of a street gang vow to take a stand against the invaders, but are unprepared for the shocking truth behind their enemies' masks. Horror, starring Sarah Akokhia and Ethan Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Deep End (1970)
programme.desc: A 15-year-old boy gets a job working at a swimming pool, and discovers his work includes providing sexual services to the patrons in exchange for tips. He lusts obsessively after a woman who works alongside him, and begins plotting to break up her engagement. Comedy drama, starring John Moulder-Brown and Jane Asher.(n)

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part.(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Comprehensive guide to who is eating what and where in the capital.(n)

programme.title: A London Community in Lockdown
programme.desc: A heartfelt look at life during lockdown in the 'Listraban' community in London, a record of a moment in time that will be in the history books for generations to come.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: First episode of the animated series about a pop group's adventures during a world tour. The girls break down in Transylvania and seek shelter in a mad scientist's castle.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.sub-title: Close Encounters of the K3 Kind
programme.desc: The band helps a young fan to drum up publicity for a struggling diner by faking an alien encounter.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar receives a message in a bottle containing a plea for help from a damsel in distress, and Vic realises the ship is infested with woodworm.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The crew is kidnapped by a tribe of British warrior women, who force Halvar to prove he is innocent of plundering their kingdom by undertaking four challenges.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer eats all the mangos on the island, so Zigby and his friends set sail in search of more.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: Everyone thinks a Bigfoot has stolen Lawrence's special shell when it disappears.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack, Ah Chu and Tashi find a haunted house in the forest, where Little Sister is hiding a tiger cub. However, she soon finds that others are looking for her new pet.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: BabaYaga offers to magically turn Jack into a great singer - but the spell goes wrong and he ends up swapping voices with a pig.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home.(n)

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A cross-border collie suffers a setback when a disc from its spine blows and Noel Fitzpatrick designs a special bolt to substitute it. A four-year-old cat is taken to the surgery after being hit by a car, and it is bad news for the owners when the X-rays reveal its pelvis is badly damaged and the operation may cost thousands of pounds.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part.(n)

programme.title: IRF Rafting World Championships
programme.desc: Over 300 of the world's best white water rafters travelled to the Cassowary Coast for a chance to become the ' best of the best' in the International Rafting Federation's premier event of 2019.(n)

programme.title: The Fast Lady (1963)
programme.desc: A cycling enthusiast on a modest civil servant's salary gives up his beloved bike to a buy a luxury car to impress a local magistrate's daughter. However, he first has to pass his driving test to stand any chance of winning her heart - and in the process ends up in a wild car-chase after a gang of robbers. Comedy, with Julie Christie, Stanley Baker, James Robertson Justice, Leslie Phillips and Dick Emery.(n)

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home.(n)

programme.title: May to December
programme.sub-title: What Kind of Fool Am I?
programme.desc: Zoe's divorce comes through, and while Alec is keen to celebrate, she is not so sure.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Jimmy Carr and the Science of Laughter - A Horizon Special
programme.desc: The comedian takes over the science programme in a special episode for BBC's sitcom season, turning it into a chat show with eminent scientists as guests. He talks to them about the nature of laughter, with the studio audiences serving as guinea pigs in his attempt to get to the bottom of what laughter is, what it has to do with comedy, and why human beings find it a pleasurable sensation. The programme reveals that laughter predates the human species and may have played a vital role in our evolution.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden.(n)

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard O'Mahoney explores the media's influence on building male celebrities.(n)

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham.(n)

programme.title: Europe at Sea ()
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the highest echelons of the European Union and their reaction to a changing world order.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which 30 recurring players compete for a daily prize of up to £1,500 and the chance to win £10,000. Every right answer takes them a step closer to a shot at that jackpot - but a single 'impossible' answer knocks them out for the day.(n)
